items.title: Rats show empathy, according to model
items.excerpt: A rat first frees a cagemate rat and then shares food with it. Is this animal just as empathetic as humans? In an American study from 2011, researchers observed that rats first freed their fellow rats from a cage and then shared food with them instead of leaving them in the cage and eating alone…

items.title: Spring songbirds may help pollinate UK trees, with pollen on 89% of studied birds
items.excerpt: The warblers—those harbingers of spring in the U.K.—have an unappreciated talent of a different sort. Common U.K. birds, including chiffchaffs and blackcaps, are playing a real role in the pollination of plants, according to new research in the Journal of Ecology.

items.title: Deuterium in comets tells interesting tales
items.excerpt: Comets have played an interesting role in the history of astronomy. Since antiquity, many cultures saw them as omens or spirits, portending good or bad news for kings, queens and emperors. Over the past few hundred years, however, astronomers have studied them intently to understand the science…

items.title: European cities short on shade as heat waves hit, urban mapping reveals
items.excerpt: More than four in five homes and workplaces across 25 European cities have less nearby tree canopy than what is needed for meaningful cooling, according to an open-data analysis by an urban greening expert.

items.title: Mars express captures dozens of dust devils in Mars valley
items.excerpt: Dust devils are a regular feature on Mars. Just like those found on Earth, these mini whirlwinds form when parts of Mars are warmed by the sun, causing air above the surface to swirl upward and carry dust with it. But in Mars' lower gravity (38% that of Earth), these devils grow much larger…

items.title: A nearby black hole as a window into the early universe

Although it is only 1.8 billion light-years away from us, the central…

items.title: Laughter may date back 15 million years, shared by humans and great apes

items.title: Japan space probe skims asteroid in test for planetary defense
items.excerpt: A Japanese space probe performed a flyby of a near-Earth asteroid on Sunday in a test mission for technology that could help protect the planet from space rocks.
